notes:
Force Proton for this title in order to download the Windows version. After that, rename arma3launcher.exe to anything and arma_x64.exe to arma3launcher.exe. The game now runs smoothly. In order to add full mod support, follow the tips in this thread.

title: Arma 3

notes: Out of the box, the game does not launch through the launcher and I had to rename arma3_x64.exe to arma3launcher.exe to bypass the launcher. When in the game the sound does not work in the game or in any menus, to fix this I had to install protontricks and use "protontricks 107410 xact" to install all versions of the xact libraries, after this the game worked flawlessly without needing to do any further changes, all graphics options work and performance is a lot better than the native port (1.82) mostly because LOD switching was working a lot better.

notes:
Native Arma 3 runs on old version and doesn't have launcher so you can't really play with mods.
However, if you install Arma via proton, you can play on last version with all the mods (except ACE).
Here is awesome guide on how to run it: https://youtu.be/swJPic2eKGA
title: Arma 3

notes:
I initially launched Arma 3 with no mods. I normally play on Takistan but I decided to try some Dynamic Recon Ops on Altis. There were quite a few frame drops especially when flying. 

I then decided to toggle my mods. I was quite worried about configuring them by hand, but this tool did it all for me: https://github.com/muttleyxd/arma3-unix-launcher

Dynamic Recon Ops on Takistan ran perfectly (There was no noticeable difference between performance on Linux and Windows on that map).

notes: The only issue I had was on the first two launches it launched on my secondary monitor. Changed monitor designations and it launched on the second one again. I launched a few other games to see if it was Steam or Arma, other games worked fine, wen't back to Arma and it launched perfectly. Haven't had any issues since but have only played singleplayer.
title: Arma 3

notes:
I've played in single player in previous versions of proton. I could categorically say battle eye did not work in those versions as I tried and was promptly kicked. I remember reading a post on reddit about how the newest version of proton made some improvements to how libraries were loaded which could improve anticheat software compatibility, so naturally I decided to give it another go

I can confirm that if you rename the arma3battleeye.exe file to arma3launcher.exe then you can in fact run multiplayer with battleeye. Performance is about the same as Windows in the sense that whatever fps you get on single player you can expect to get about 10-20 less on multiplayer. But it's totally playable and fine.

It might be risky though as this isn't officially supported by battleeye, bohemia, or valve so if you get banned don't blame me! :P
title: Arma 3

notes:
Simply rename arma3battleye.exe to arma3launcher.exe, and you are set. If you wish to run Teamspeak with your other mods, like TFAR, download the windows version of TFAR, and then run WINE from protonfolder/dist/bin/wine to install it and such.

This is a great week for Linux gaming.

title: Arma 3

notes: -noesync -noLauncher Played online, worked for about 15 minutes like everyone else has said then crashed. I played the main campaign, completed first 3 missions plus a stress test, didn't crash for over 3 hours. Performance is on par with Windows. Make sure to install this game to your SSD for a performance gain. This game is now more stable via Proton then it's native port was. Will test more in the future, hopefully when Battleeye is fully working.
title: Arma 3

notes:
Works however as people have pointed out you need -noLauncher to start the game, and when joining a MP server for first time it will prompt to install anticheat software which installs fine, you then need to relaunch game and join server again. 
However it will crash after 15minutes, hopefully that can be fixed at some point.

Also this works fine with esync from my testing, and to resolve the mouse panning to right lock issue if you get that, is set display to fullscreen window (only happens on fullscreen).
title: Arma 3

notes:
Alright, Arma3 works fine no issue's on that department for performance that I can see, but BattlEye is still a little messy, when closed. I still need system monitor to close the process of BattlEye.

The work-around is still needed "-noLauncher" in properties.
The bug of 15 minutes still is an issue, rather annoying to be honest.
Then when you leave a match to disconnect the game also freezes.
And when on fullscreen, you still need to tab out, then tab back in to get the mouse turn/aim unstuck from the right side of the screen.
